children.heading: .—Establishment
children.label: a .—Establishment
children.text: There is established in the Treasury of the United States a fund to be known as the “National Parks and Public Land Legacy Restoration Fund”.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), for each of fiscal years 2021 through 2025, there shall be deposited in the Fund an amount equal to 50 percent of all energy development revenues due and payable to the United States from oil, gas, coal, or alternative or renewable energy development on Federal land and water credited, covered, or deposited as miscellaneous receipts under Federal law in the preceding fiscal year.

children.heading: .—Maximum amount
children.label: 2 .—Maximum amount
children.text: The amount deposited in the Fund under paragraph (1) shall not exceed $1,900,000,000 for any fiscal year.

children.label: A
children.text: are due to the United States, special funds, trust funds, or States from mineral and energy development on Federal land and water; or

children.label: I
children.text: the Gulf of Mexico Energy Security Act of 2006 ( note; ); and43 U.S.C. 1331Public Law 109–432

children.label: II
children.text: the Mineral Leasing Act ( et seq.); or30 U.S.C. 181

children.label: I
children.text: the Land and Water Conservation Fund established under chapter 2003; or

children.label: II
children.text: the Historic Preservation Fund established under chapter 3031.

children.heading: .—Availability of Funds
children.label: c .—Availability of Funds
children.text: Amounts deposited in the Fund shall be available to the Secretary and the Secretary of Agriculture, as provided in subsection (e), without further appropriation or fiscal year limitation.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary may request the Secretary of the Treasury to invest any portion of the Fund that is not, as determined by the Secretary, in consultation with the Secretary of Agriculture, required to meet the current needs of the Fund.

children.label: A
children.text: with a maturity suitable to the needs of the Fund, as determined by the Secretary; and

children.label: B
children.text: bearing interest at a rate determined by the Secretary of the Treasury, taking into consideration current market yields on outstanding marketable obligations of the United States of comparable maturity.

children.heading: .—Credits to fund
children.label: 3 .—Credits to fund
children.text: The income on investments of the Fund under this subsection shall be credited to, and form a part of, the Fund.

children.label: A
children.text: 70 percent of the amounts deposited in the Fund for each fiscal year shall be allocated to the Service.

children.label: B
children.text: 15 percent of the amounts deposited in the Fund for each fiscal year shall be allocated to the Forest Service.

children.label: C
children.text: 5 percent of the amounts deposited in the Fund for each fiscal year shall be allocated to the United States Fish and Wildlife Service.

children.label: D
children.text: 5 percent of the amounts deposited in the Fund for each fiscal year shall be allocated to the Bureau of Land Management.

children.label: E
children.text: 5 percent of the amounts deposited in the Fund for each fiscal year shall be allocated to the Bureau of Indian Education.

children.heading: .—Non-transportation projects
children.label: A .—Non-transportation projects
children.text: Over the term of the Fund, within each covered agency, not less than 65 percent of amounts from the Fund shall be allocated for non-transportation projects.

children.heading: .—Transportation projects
children.label: B .—Transportation projects
children.text: The amounts remaining in the Fund after the allocations required under subparagraph (A) may be allocated for transportation projects of the covered agencies, including paved and unpaved roads, bridges, tunnels, and paved parking areas.

children.heading: .—Plan
children.label: C .—Plan
children.text: Any priority deferred maintenance project funded under this section shall be consistent with an applicable transportation, deferred maintenance, or capital improvement plan developed by the applicable covered agency.
for land acquisition;
to supplant discretionary funding made available for annually recurring facility operations, maintenance, and construction needs; or
for bonuses for employees of the Federal Government that are carrying out this section.

children.heading: .—Submission of Priority List of Projects to Congress
children.label: g .—Submission of Priority List of Projects to Congress

children.text: are identified by the Secretary and the Secretary of Agriculture as priority deferred maintenance projects; and
as of the date of the submission of the list, are ready to be implemented.

children.heading: .—Submission of Annual List of Projects to Congress
children.label: h .—Submission of Annual List of Projects to Congress
children.text: Until the date on which all of the amounts in the Fund are expended, the President shall annually submit to Congress, together with the annual budget of the United States, a list of projects to be funded from the Fund that includes a detailed description of each project, including the estimated expenditures from the Fund for the project for the applicable fiscal year.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Appropriations Acts may provide for alternate allocation of amounts made available under this section, consistent with the allocations to covered agencies under subsection (e)(1).

children.heading: .—No alternate allocations
children.label: A .—No alternate allocations
children.text: If Congress has not enacted legislation establishing alternate allocations by the date on which the Act making full-year appropriations for the Department of the Interior, Environment, and Related Agencies for the applicable fiscal year is enacted into law, amounts made available under subsection (c) shall be allocated by the President.

children.heading: .—Insufficient alternate allocation
children.label: B .—Insufficient alternate allocation
children.text: If Congress enacts legislation establishing alternate allocations for amounts made available under subsection (c) that are less than the full amount appropriated under that subsection, the difference between the amount appropriated and the alternate allocation shall be allocated by the President.

children.label: A
children.text: to reduce the deferred maintenance backlog; and

children.label: B
children.text: to encourage relevant public-private partnerships.

children.label: A
children.text: credited to, and form a part of, the Fund; and

children.label: B
children.text: allocated to the covered agency for which the donation was made.

children.heading: .—Other allocations
children.label: 3 .—Other allocations
children.text: Any donations allocated to a covered agency under paragraph (2)(B) shall be allocated to the applicable covered agency independently of the allocations under subsection (e)(1).

children.heading: .—Required Consideration for Accessibility
children.label: k .—Required Consideration for Accessibility
children.text: In expending amounts from the Fund, the Secretary and the Secretary of Agriculture shall incorporate measures to improve the accessibility of assets and accommodate visitors and employees with disabilities in accordance with applicable law.
